title: agoseris glauca var. laciniata
intro: Agoseris glauca var. laciniata, also known as the laciniated agoseris or laciniated milky spore, is a rare and endangered flowering plant native to the western United States. It has been used in traditional medicine for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory and antiseptic compounds. The plant's roots and leaves have been reported to have health benefits such as treating fever, rheumatism, and skin conditions, and it is also used as a diuretic to treat urinary problems. Herbal preparations of Agoseris glauca var. laciniata are typically made by infusing the roots and leaves in hot water or by using them in teas and tinctures, although its use should be carefully considered due to its rarity and potential toxicity.
